author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVII. 62-64.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate, saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while He was yet alive,After three days I willrise again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest His disciples come and steal Him away, and say to the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error should beworse than the first.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Everywhere deceit recoils upon itself, and against its will supports the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And observe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was necessary for it to be believed that He died, and that He rose again, and that He was buried, and all these things are brought to pass by His enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, at any rate, these words bearing witness to every one of these facts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We remember,” these are the words, “that that deceiver said, when He was yet alive,” (He was therefore now dead), “After three days I rise again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Command therefore that the sepulchre be sealed,” (He was therefore buried), “lest His disciples come and steal Him away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if the sepulchre be sealed, there will be no unfair dealing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there could not be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So then the proof of His resurrection has become incontrovertible by what ye have put forward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because it was sealed, there was no unfair dealing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if there was no unfair dealing, and the sepulchre was found empty, it is manifest that He is risen, plainly and incontrovertibly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how even against their will they contend for the proof of the truth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, the disciples’ love of truth, how they conceal from us none of the things that are said by His enemies, though they use opprobrious language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold, at any rate, they even call Him a deceiver, and these men are not silent about that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things show also their savageness (that not even at His death did they let go their anger), and these men’s simple and truthful disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it were worth while to inquire concerning that point also, where He said, “After three days I rise again?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one would not find this thus distinctly stated,but rather the example of Jonah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that they understood His saying, and of their own will dealt unfairly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Pilate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye have a watch; make it as sure as ye can.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they made it sure, sealing the sepulchre, and setting the watch.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He suffers not the soldiers alone to seal, for as having learnt the things concerning Christ, he was no longer willing to co-operate with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in order to be rid of them, he endures this also, and saith, “Do ye seal it as ye will, that ye may not have it in your power to blame others.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the soldiers only had sealed, they might have said (although the saying would have been improbable and false, yet nevertheless as in the rest they cast aside shame, so in this too they might have been able to say), that the soldiers, having given up the body to be stolen, gave His disciples opportunity to feign the history concerning His resurrection, but now having themselves made it sure, they are not able to say so much as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how they labor for the truth against their will?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they themselves came to Pilate, themselves asked, themselves sealed, setting the watch, so as to be accusers, and refuters one of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed when should they have stolen Him? on the Sabbath?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how? for it was not lawful so much as to go out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And even if they transgressed the law, how should they have dared, who were so timid, to come forth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how could they also have been able to persuade the multitude?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By saying what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By doing what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And from what sort of zeal could they have stood in behalf of the dead? expecting what recompense? what requital?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seeing Him yet alive and merely seized, they had fled; and after His death were they likely to speak boldly in His behalf, unless He had risen again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how should these things be reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they were neither willing nor able to feign a resurrection, that did not take place, is plain from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He discoursed to them much of a resurrection, and continually said, as indeed these very men have stated, “After three days I rise again.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If therefore He rose not again, it is quite clear that these men (having been deceived and made enemies to an entire nation for His sake, and come to be without home and without city) would have abhorred Him, and would not have been willing to invest Him with such glory; as having been deceived, and having fallen into the utmost dangers on His account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they would not even have been able, unless the resurrection had been true, to feign it, this does not so much as need reasoning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in what were they confident?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the shrewdness of their reasonings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay of all men they were the most unlearned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in the abundance of their possessions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, they had neither staff nor shoes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in the distinction of their race?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, they were mean, and of mean ancestors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in the greatness of their country?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, they were of obscure places.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in their own numbers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, they were not more than eleven, and they were scattered abroad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in their Master’s promises?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What kind of promises?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He were not risen again, neither would those be likely to be trusted by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how should they endure a frantic people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the chief of them endured not the speech of a woman, keeping the door, and if all the rest too, on seeing Him bound, were scattered abroad, how should they have thought to run to the ends of the earth, and plant a feigned tale of a resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he stood not a woman’s threat, and they not so much as the sight of bonds, how were they able to stand against kings, and rulers, and nations, where were swords, and gridirons, and furnaces, and ten thousand deaths day by day, unless they had the benefit of the power and graceof Him who rose again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such miracles and so many were done, and none of these things did the Jews regard, but crucified Him, who had done them, and were they likely to believe these men at their mere word about a resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things are not, they are not so, but the might of Him, who rose again, brought them to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, their craft, how ridiculous it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We remember,” these are their words, “that that deceiver said, while He was yet alive, After three days I rise again.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet if He were a deceiver, and boastfully uttered falsehood, why are ye afraid and run to and fro, and use so much diligence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We are afraid, it is replied, lest perchance the disciples steal Him away, and deceive the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet this has been proved to have no probability at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Malice, however, is a thing contentious and shameless, and attempts what is unreasonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they command it to be made sure for three days, as contending for doctrines, and being minded to prove that before that time also He was a deceiver, and they extend their malice even to His tomb.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason then He rose sooner, that they might not say that He spake falsely, and was stolen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this, His rising sooner, was open to no charge, but to be later would have been full of suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed if He had not risen then, when they were sitting there, and watching, but when they had withdrawn after the three days, they would have had something to say, and to speak against it, although foolishly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason then He anticipated the time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was meet the resurrection should take place, while they were sitting by and watching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also it was fit it should take place within the three days, since if it had been when they were passed, and the men had withdrawn, the matter would have been regarded with suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He allowed them to seal it, as they were minded, and soldiers sat around it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they cared not about doing these things, and working on a Sabbath day, but they looked to one object only, their own wicked purpose, as though by that they were to succeed; which was a mark of extreme folly, and of fear now greatly dismaying them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they who seized Him, when living, are afraid of Him when dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if He had been a mere man, they had reason to have taken courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that they might learn, that when living also He endured of His own will, what He did endure; behold, both a seal, a stone, and a watch, and they were not able to hold Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there was one result only, that the burial was published, and the resurrection thereby proved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed soldiers sat by it, and Jews are on the watch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But in the end of the Sabbath,as it began to dawn towards the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And behold there was a great earthquake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For an angel of the Lord descended from Heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door of the tomb,and sat upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After the resurrection came the angel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then came he, and took away the stone?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of the women, for they themselves had seen Him then in the sepulchre.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore that they might believe that He was risen again, they see the sepulchre void of the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause he removed the stone, for this cause also an earthquake took place, that they might be thoroughly aroused and awakened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they were come to pour oil on Him, and these things were done at night, and it is likely that some also had become drowsy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for what intent and cause doth he say, “Fear not ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First he delivers them from the dread, and then tells them of the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the ye is of one showing them great honor, and indicating, that extreme punishment awaits them that had dared to do, what the others had dared, except they repented.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to be afraid is not for you, he means, but for them that crucified Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having delivered them then from the fear both by his words, and by his appearance (for his form he showed bright, as bearing such good tidings), he went on to say, “I know that ye seek Jesus the Crucified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he is not ashamed to call Him “crucified;” for this is the chief of the blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He is risen.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence is it evident?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “As He said.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if ye refuse to believe me, he would say, remember His words, and neither will ye disbelieve me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then also another proof, “Come and see the place where He lay.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this he had lifted up the stone, in order that from this too they might receive the proof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And tell His disciples, that ye shall see Him in Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he prepares them to bear good tidings to others, which thing most of all made them believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said well “in Galilee,” freeing them from troubles and dangers, so that fear should not hinder their faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And they departed from the sepulchre with fear and joy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why could this be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They had seen a thing amazing, and beyond expectation, a tomb empty, where they had before seen Him laid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He had led them to the sight, that they might become witnesses of both things, both of His tomb, and of His resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they considered that no man could have taken Him, when so many soldiers were sitting by Him, unless He raised up Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause also they rejoice and wonder, and receive the reward of so much continuance with Him, that they should first see and gladly declare, not what had been said only, but also what they beheld.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore after then they had departed with fear and joy, “Behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But “they held Him by the feet,”and with exceeding joy and gladness ran unto Him, and received by the touch also, an infallible proof, and full assurance of the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And they worshipped Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Be not afraid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, He Himself casts out their fear, making way for faith, “But go, tell my brethren, that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark how He Himself sends good tidings to His disciples by these women, bringing to honor, as I have often said, that sex, which was most dishonored, and to good hopes; and healing that which was diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Perchance some one of you would wish to be like them, to hold the feet of Jesus; ye can even now, and not His feet and His hands only, but even lay hold on that sacred head, receiving the awful mysteries with a pure conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not here only, but also in that day ye shall see Him, coming with that unspeakable glory, and the multitude of the angels, if ye are disposed to be humane; and ye shall hear not these words only, “All hail!” but also those others, “Come ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you before the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be ye therefore humane, that ye may hear these things; and ye women, that wear gold, who have looked on the running of these women, at last, though late, lay aside the disease of the desire for golden ornaments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if ye are emulous of these women, change the ornaments which ye wear, and clothe yourselves instead with almsgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is the use, I pray you, of these precious stones, and of the garments spangled with gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “My soul,” you say, “is glad, and is pleased with these things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I asked thee the profit, but thou tellest me the hurt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing is worse than being taken up with these things, and delighting in them, and being riveted to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For more bitter is this grievous slavery, when any one finds delight even in being a slave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in what spiritual matter will she ever be diligent as she ought; when will she laugh to scorn, as she should, the things of this world, who thinks it a worthy matter for joy, that she hath been chained in gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that continues in prison, and is pleased, will never desire to be set free; as indeed neither will this woman; but as having become a kind of captive to this wicked desire, she will not endure so much as to hear spiritual language with becoming desire and diligence, much less to engage in such work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the profit of these ornaments and this luxury?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I pray thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I am pleased with them,” thou sayest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again thou hast told of the hurt and the ruin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But I enjoy also,” thou sayest, “much honor from the beholders.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the occasion of another destruction, when thou art lifted up to haughtiness, to arrogance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come now, since thou hast not told me of the profit, bear with me while I tell thee of the mischiefs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then are the mischiefs resulting therefrom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Anxiety, which is greater than the pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore many of the beholders, these I mean of the grosser sort, derive more pleasure from it than she who wears the gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou indeed deckest thyself with anxiety, but they, without this, feast their eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, there are other things again, the debasing of the soul, the being looked upon with envy on all sides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the neighboring women stung by it, arm themselves against their own husbands, and stir up against thee grievous wars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Together with these things, the fact that all one’s leisure and anxiety are spent on this object, that one doth not apply one’s self earnestly to spiritual achievements; that one is filled with haughtiness, arrogance, and vainglory; that one is riveted to the earth, and loses one’s wings, and instead of an eagle, becometh a dog or a swine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having given up looking up into Heaven, and flying thither, thou bendest down to the earth like the swine, being curious about mines and caverns, and having an unmanly and base soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But dost thou, when thou appearest, turn towards thee the eyes of them at the market-place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well then; for this very reason, thou shouldest not wear gold, that thou mayest not become a common gazing stock, and open the mouths of many accusers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For none of those whose eyes are toward thee admireth thee, but they jeer at thee, as fond of dress, as boastful, as a carnal woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And shouldest thou enter into a church, thou goest forth, without getting anything but countless leers, and revilings, and curses, not from the beholders only, but also from the prophet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For straightway Isaiah,that hath the fullest voice of all, as soon as he hath seen thee, will cry out, “These things saith the Lord against the princely daughters of Sion; because they walked with a lofty neck, and with winkings of the eyes, and in their walking, trailing their garments, and mincing at the same time with their feet; the Lord shall take off their bravery, and instead of a sweet smell there shall be dust, and instead of a stomacher, thou shalt gird thyself with a cord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things for thy gorgeous array.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not to them only are these words addressed, but to every woman that doeth like them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul again with him stands as an accuser, telling Timothy to charge the women, “not to adorn themselves with braided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that everywhere the wearing of gold is hurtful, but especially when thou art entering into a church, when thou passest through the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou wert exceedingly anxious to bring an accusation against thyself, thou couldest not put on any other array than this visage of cruelty and inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider at any rate how many hungry bellies thou passest by with this array, how many naked bodies with this satanical display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How much better to feed hungry souls, than to bore through the lobes of thy ears, and to hang from them the food of countless poor for no purpose or profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? is to be rich a commendation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? is to wear gold a praise?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though it be from honest earnings that these things are put on you, even so what thou hast done is a very heavy charge against thee; but when it is moreover from dishonesty, consider the exceeding greatness of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But dost thou love praises and honor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Strip thyself therefore of this ridiculous clothing, and then all will admire thee; then shalt thou enjoy both honor and pure pleasure; since now at any rate thou art overwhelmed with jeers, working for thyself many causes of vexation arising out of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For should any of these things be missing, consider how many are the evils that have their birth therefrom, how many maidservants are beaten, how many men put to trouble, how many led to execution, how many cast into prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And trials arise hence, and actions, and countless curses and accusations against the wife from the husband, against the hus band from her friends, against the soul from itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But it will not be lost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the first place, this is not easy to secure, but even if it be kept safe constantly, yet by being kept, it occasions much anxiety and care and discomfort, and no advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what kind of profit arises from hence to the house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What advantage to the woman herself who wears it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No advantage indeed, but much unseemliness, and accusation from every quarter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How wilt thou be able to kiss Christ’s feet, and cling to them, when thus dressed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From this adorning He turneth away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause He vouchsafed to be born in the house of the carpenter, or rather not even in that house, but in a shed, and a manger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then wilt thou be able to behold Him, not having beauty that is desirable in His eyes, not wearing the array that is lovely before Him, but what is hateful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that cometh unto Him must not deck himself out with such garments, but be clothed with virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider what after all these jewels are.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nothing else than earth and ashes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mix water with them, and they are clay.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider and be ashamed to make clay thy master, forsaking all, and abiding by it, and carrying and bearing it about, even when thou enterest into a church, when most of all thou oughtest to flee from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither for this cause was the church built, that thou shouldest display therein these riches, but spiritual riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, as though thou wert entering into a pompous procession, thus deckest thyself out on every side, imitating the women on the stage, even so dost thou carry about in profusion that ridiculous mass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, I tell thee, thou comest for mischief to many, and when the congregation is dismissed, in their houses, at their tables, one may hear the more part describing these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they have left off saying, thus and thus said the prophet and the apostle, and they describe the costliness of your garments, the size of your precious stones, and all the other unseemliness of them that wear these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This makes you backward in almsgiving, and your husbands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one of you would not readily consent to break up one of these ornaments to feed a poor man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when thou wouldest choose even thyself to be in distress rather than to behold these things broken to pieces, how shouldest thou feed another at the cost of them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For most women feel towards these things, as to some living beings, and not less than towards their children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “God forbid,” thou sayest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Prove me this then, prove it by your works, as now at least I see the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who ever of those that are completely taken captive, by melting down these things, would rescue a child’s soul from death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I say a child’s?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath redeemed his own soul thereby, when perishing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, on the contrary, the more part even set it to sale for these things every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And should any bodily infirmity take place, they do everything, but if they see their soul depraved, they take no such pains, but are careless both about their children’s soul, and their own soul, in order that these things may remain to rust with time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whilst thou art wearing jewels worth ten thousand talents, the member of Christ hath not the enjoyment so much as of necessary food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas the common Lord of all hath imparted to all alike of heaven, and of the things in Heaven, and of the spiritual table, thou dost not impart to Him even of perishing things, on purpose that thou mayest continue perpetually bound with these grievous chains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence the countless evils,hence the fornications of the men, when ye prepare them to cast off self-restraint, when ye teach them to take delight in these things with which the harlot women deck themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause they are so quickly taken captive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou hadst instructed him to look down upon these things, and to take delight in chastity, godly fear and humility, he would not have been so easily taken by the shaftsof fornication.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the harlot is able to adorn herself in this way even to a greater degree than this, but with those other ornaments not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accustom him then to take delight in these ornaments, which he cannot see placed on the harlot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how wilt thou bring him into this habit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou take off these, and put on those others, so shall both thy husband be in safety, and thou in honor, and God will be propitious to you, and all men will admire you, and ye will attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might, world without end.
